metrics.description: Canonical reduced-bias DSP: phase-1 premium only multiplies the benefit signal.
metrics.optimum_message: CONVERGENCE: NORM OF PROJECTED GRADIENT <= PGTOL

metrics.description: Empirical comparator: phase-1 multiplier enters exposure for benefit and penalty.
metrics.optimum_message: CONVERGENCE: NORM OF PROJECTED GRADIENT <= PGTOL

metrics.description: Decouples phase-1 benefit, saturation, and penalty multipliers as separate global scalars.
metrics.optimum_message: CONVERGENCE: NORM OF PROJECTED GRADIENT <= PGTOL

metrics.description: Unties effective exposure into separate saturation and penalty multipliers, with no benefit premium.
metrics.optimum_message: CONVERGENCE: NORM OF PROJECTED GRADIENT <= PGTOL

metrics.description: Apple-style repetition-aware DSP: per-domain r1 discounts physical repetition for saturation; phase-1 only changes benefit amplitude and penalty uses raw physical exposure.
metrics.optimum_message: CONVERGENCE: RELATIVE REDUCTION OF F <= FACTR*EPSMCH

metrics.description: RAML-DSP: shared r1 turns physical exposure into the benefit feature directly; phase-1 only changes benefit amplitude and penalty uses raw exposure.
metrics.optimum_message: CONVERGENCE: NORM OF PROJECTED GRADIENT <= PGTOL

metrics.description: RAML-DSP: per-domain r1 turns physical exposure into the benefit feature directly; phase-1 only changes benefit amplitude and penalty uses raw exposure.
metrics.optimum_message: CONVERGENCE: NORM OF PROJECTED GRADIENT <= PGTOL

metrics.description: RAML-DSP diagnostic: per-domain r1 drives both benefit exposure and penalty exposure, testing whether overexposure should decay with effective repetition.
metrics.optimum_message: CONVERGENCE: NORM OF PROJECTED GRADIENT <= PGTOL
